title: pyrus lutea
intro: Pyrus lutea, also known as the golden pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is native to Western China. The medicinal properties of Pyrus lutea lie in its bark, which contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of consuming or applying Pyrus lutea preparations include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Pyrus lutea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, or it can be used in traditional medicine to create remedies such as cough syrups and lozenges.
